city_report.transport: Regular Warsaw transport tickets issued by ZTM (the local transport operator which runs city buses, trams and the metro) are valid on SKM train services between the airport and Warsaw. The airport is in fare zone 1 so a single adult ride is 3.40 zł. If you need a bus at the other end, get a one-transfer ticket (valid 75 min) for 4.40 zł.

attraction.name: Royal Castle
attraction.description: Reconstructed castle and former royal residence.

attraction.name: Old Town Market
attraction.description: The historical heart and oldest part of Warsaw.

attraction.name: Lazienki Park
attraction.description: Largest park with a palace on the water.

attraction.name: Palace of Culture
attraction.description: Iconic socialist-realist skyscraper and city landmark.

attraction.name: Wilanow Palace
attraction.description: Baroque royal palace survived the wars.

attraction.name: Uprising Museum
attraction.description: Museum dedicated to the Warsaw Uprising of 1944.

attraction.name: Vistula Boulevards
attraction.description: Modern riverside promenade with bars and views.

attraction.name: POLIN Museum
attraction.description: Museum of the History of Polish Jews.

attraction.name: Saxon Garden
attraction.description: Oldest public park in the city.

attraction.name: Praga District
attraction.description: Artistic district on the right bank of the Vistula.

city_report.description: Esempio straordinario di ricostruzione e resilienza, Varsavia è oggi una metropoli verticale e dinamica. Combina un settore finanziario in crescita con ampi spazi verdi e una sicurezza urbana migliorata.
city_report.wiki_intro: '''Warsaw''' (Polish phrasebook|Polish: ''Warszawa'') is Poland's capital and largest city. Warsaw is a bustling metropolis and one of the European Union's fastest-developing capitals and the Union's ninth most populous urban centre. It has a mixture of new and old in its eclectic architectural mix, and is constantly changing. There is a large variety of museums, galleries and other tourist at...
